headlines.title: Oil Supply Shock Worsens amid Plunging Petroleum Inventories
headlines.summary: Global crude oil and fuel inventories are crashing at a record speed as the supply shock from the Middle East is too big to absorb without stock depletion. While the futures markets trade on sentiment and hopes that a U.S.-Iran deal could soon lead to the reopening of the Strait

headlines.title: EU: Airlines Should Pay Passengers for Cancelations due to Fuel Price Surge
headlines.summary: Airlines will have to continue reimbursing travelers if they cancel flights due to high jet fuel prices, EU Sustainable Transport and Tourism Commissioner, Apostolos Tzitzikostas, told the Financial Times in an interview published on Thursday. headlines.title: Iran’s Oil Industry Under Pressure as U.S. Naval Blockade Begins to Bite
headlines.summary: An Iranian energy official just conceded something in a surprise admission that the US naval blockade has begun to bite the Islamic Republic's oil industry. headlines.title: Angola's First New Refinery in 50 Years Ships Its First Fuel Cargoes
headlines.summary: The Cabinda oil refinery in Angola, the first that was built in the country since it gained independence from Portugal 50 years ago, has started shipping fuels to the domestic and international markets, in a relief to the fuel supply stress due to the Iran war. headlines.title: EU Imports of Yamal Gas Hit Record Ahead of Ban on Russian LNG
headlines.summary: The EU boosted its imports of Russian LNG from the Yamal LNG project to a record high in the first four months of 2026, just before the bloc started implementing a phased-out ban on imports of Russia’s LNG, bne IntelliNews reported on Thursday, citing a report by environmental ca

headlines.title: Oil Prices Waver as Market Weighs Chances of U.S.-Iran Deal
headlines.summary: Front-month Brent Crude futures dropped to below $100 per barrel again in morning trade in Europe on Thursday, reversing earlier gains, as the market hopes the U.S. and Iran could be able to reach an agreement that would eventually reopen the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Oil Crunch Prompts Plastics Crisis in Asia
headlines.summary: Asia is facing plastics shortages because of the oil and gas supply crunch caused by the war in the Middle East, with medical supplies, packaging, and consumer products most vulnerable to a shock, the Financial Times has reported. headlines.title: EU Suspends Methane Rules as Energy Crunch Intensifies
headlines.summary: The European Union has decided to suspend a regulation requiring oil and gas suppliers to report emissions along their supply chains amid an energy crunch that has sent oil and gas prices higher and prompted a rush to secure scarce supply. headlines.title: Australia Orders LNG Exporters to Reserve 20% of Gas for Domestic Market
headlines.summary: Australia’s government has mandated energy companies in the country to set aside 20% of their natural gas output for the domestic market to avoid supply shortages along the east coast. headlines.title: Oil Prices Edge Higher as Iran Deal Doubts Resurface
headlines.summary: Oil prices edged higher in early Asian trade on Thursday, rebounding from the previous session’s steep selloff, as investors reassessed the chances of a near-term U.S.-Iran peace agreement and weighed continuing disruptions to Middle East energy flows.
